questions.q: In which city is the Kashi Vishwanath Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Kashi Vishwanath Temple stands in Varanasi (Kashi), Uttar Pradesh, on the banks of the Ganga.

questions.q: Which Hindu deity is worshipped at the Kashi Vishwanath Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva, worshipped here as Vishwanath, the 'Lord of the Universe'.

questions.q: On the bank of which river does the Kashi Vishwanath Temple stand?
questions.explanation: The temple lies in Varanasi on the western bank of the sacred Ganga river.

questions.q: What does the name 'Vishwanath' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Vishwanath' translates to 'Lord of the Universe', a title of Shiva venerated at this temple.

questions.q: The temple's spire and dome are famously plated with which precious metal?
questions.explanation: The temple's shikhara and dome are covered in gold, earning it the nickname the 'Golden Temple'.

questions.q: By what ancient name is Varanasi, the temple's city, also known?
questions.explanation: Varanasi is traditionally called Kashi, which is why the temple is named Kashi Vishwanath.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Kashi Vishwanath Temple situated?
questions.explanation: Varanasi and its Kashi Vishwanath Temple are located in the state of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: The Kashi Vishwanath Temple is regarded as one of the holiest sites for which religion?
questions.explanation: It is one of the most sacred pilgrimage sites in Hinduism, especially for devotees of Shiva.

questions.q: What sacred object representing Shiva is enshrined in the temple's sanctum?
options: A holy relic of the Buddha
questions.explanation: The sanctum houses a Shiva lingam venerated as one of the twelve Jyotirlingas.

questions.q: By what popular nickname is the Kashi Vishwanath Temple commonly known?
questions.explanation: Because of its gold-plated spire and dome, it is widely called the 'Golden Temple' of Shiva.

questions.q: The temple enshrines one of the twelve sacred Jyotirlingas. What is a Jyotirlinga?
options: A devotional shrine to Shiva represented as a 'pillar of light'
questions.explanation: A Jyotirlinga is a shrine where Shiva is worshipped as a luminous lingam, a 'pillar of light'.

questions.q: Which 18th-century Maratha queen rebuilt the current Kashi Vishwanath Temple in 1780?
questions.explanation: Maharani Ahilyabai Holkar of Indore commissioned the rebuilding of the present temple in 1780.

questions.q: Which Sikh ruler donated the gold used to plate the temple's spire and dome?
questions.explanation: Maharaja Ranjit Singh of the Sikh Empire donated the gold (around 1835) for the temple's plating.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor is recorded as having demolished an earlier Kashi Vishwanath Temple in 1669?
questions.explanation: The emperor Aurangzeb ordered the destruction of the earlier temple in 1669.

questions.q: Which large mosque stands immediately adjacent to the Kashi Vishwanath Temple?
questions.explanation: The Gyanvapi Mosque stands beside the temple on the site of the earlier structure.

questions.q: The name 'Gyanvapi', associated with the temple complex, refers to a sacred what?
questions.explanation: 'Gyanvapi' means 'Well of Wisdom', a revered well within the temple precinct.

questions.q: Approximately how much gold did Maharaja Ranjit Singh reportedly donate for the temple's dome?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Ranjit Singh donated roughly a tonne of gold for plating the temple.

questions.q: A modern access corridor inaugurated in 2021 connects the temple directly to which feature?
questions.explanation: The Kashi Vishwanath Dham corridor, opened in December 2021, links the temple to the Ganga ghats.

questions.q: Which ghat on the Ganga lies near the temple and is famous for Hindu cremation rituals?
questions.explanation: Manikarnika Ghat, near the temple, is among Varanasi's most sacred cremation ghats.

questions.q: Varanasi, the home of the temple, is widely described as one of the world's what?
questions.explanation: Varanasi is regarded as one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities, drawing Shiva pilgrims for millennia.

questions.q: Whom did Maharani Ahilyabai Holkar, the temple's rebuilder, primarily serve as a ruler?
options: The Maratha-ruled Malwa kingdom from Indore
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai Holkar ruled the Malwa kingdom from Maheshwar/Indore as a Maratha Holkar queen.

questions.q: Which architectural element of the temple is most prominently covered in gold?
questions.explanation: The temple's shikhara (spire) and dome are the gold-plated features that give it its golden glow.

questions.q: In which year was the present Kashi Vishwanath Temple structure rebuilt by Ahilyabai Holkar?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai Holkar had the current temple built in 1780 near the site of the earlier shrine.

questions.q: Of the twelve Jyotirlingas across India, how many are enshrined at Kashi Vishwanath?
questions.explanation: Kashi Vishwanath holds exactly one of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Shiva.

questions.q: Around which year did Maharaja Ranjit Singh provide the gold for the temple's plating?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh is recorded as having donated the gold for the temple roughly in 1835.

questions.q: The Kashi Vishwanath Dham corridor connecting the temple to the Ganga was inaugurated in which month and year?
questions.explanation: The Kashi Vishwanath Dham corridor was inaugurated in December 2021.

questions.q: An influential medieval temple at the site was reportedly constructed during Akbar's reign with the patronage of which Hindu noble?
options: Raja Man Singh / Raja Todar Mal
questions.explanation: A 16th-century temple here is associated with Raja Todar Mal (and Raja Man Singh) during Akbar's era.

questions.q: Which deity, the consort of Shiva, has a renowned temple within Varanasi closely linked to Kashi Vishwanath pilgrimage?
questions.explanation: The Annapurna Devi temple, dedicated to Shiva's consort as the goddess of nourishment, adjoins the Kashi Vishwanath pilgrimage circuit.

questions.q: What is the approximate total weight of gold (in earlier and later donations) said to adorn the temple's spire and dome?
options: Roughly several hundred kilograms to about a tonne
questions.explanation: The gold adorning the temple is traditionally estimated in the range of several hundred kilograms up to about a tonne.

questions.q: Pilgrimage to Kashi Vishwanath is traditionally believed by devotees to grant what spiritual outcome?
options: Moksha (liberation from the cycle of rebirth)
questions.explanation: Hindu tradition holds that worship and death in Kashi grant moksha, liberation from the cycle of rebirth.
